briefing.headline: Rig up a Football Jig — steady summer approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Football Jig is a strong choice for summer with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: When fish are stuck to the bottom in heavy current, snap a 3/4-oz football jig — not stroking it (for suspended fish) but a small snap, let it fall, snap, let it fall. This makes pressured, bottom-hugging fish react

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: When fish are stuck to the bottom in heavy current, snap a 3/4-oz football jig — not stroking it (for suspended fish) but a small snap, let it fall, snap, let it fall. This makes pressured, b
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: A snapped football jig catches fish from pressured busted-up schools that won't bite other techniques — especially effective without FFS, just covering water with precise casts
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, flat, point. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: Snapping a football jig is different from stroking — stroking is for suspended fish, snapping is a small sharp motion that makes bottom-hugging fish in heavy current react. The higher-frequency kick action of the Cleanup Craw trailer comp...

Johnston: Nobody remembers who finishes second in the Classic, so throw big baits and go for the win. Expect big swimbaits, deep-diving crankbaits, Carolina rigs, and football jigs to be the best chances.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites are subtle, try a lighter jig weight — slower fall rate can be the difference
